content: Health aid impact assessment shows mixed results: Successful areas - vaccination campaigns (Gavi estimated to prevent 15 million deaths by 2030), HIV treatment scale-up (PEPFAR supporting 20 million on ART), malaria control (bed nets reducing child mortality 20%). Limited success areas - maternal mortality (slow progress despite investment), health system strengthening (difficult to attribute outcomes), sustainability after aid withdrawal. Cost-effectiveness varies widely: bed nets $50-100 per life-year saved, ART $300-500 per life-year in low-income settings, versus some health system investments lacking clear metrics. Aid dependency concerns: Sub-Saharan African countries receive 20-50% of health funding from external sources, creating vulnerability to donor priority shifts. Transition planning essential as countries graduate from low-income status.
